Eventually ports to its own repo. No timeline yet — research-first.

title: Todo JSONL data loss prevention — Write tool overwrites silently drop entries
description: The /todo skill uses Write tool to overwrite the entire todos.jsonl file. If a session reads the file before recent entries exist (e.g., after compaction loses context of a prior write), then writes, old entries are silently dropped. This already happened twice — T26/T27/T28 were lost and had to be recovered from git history (commit 24e7af36 and Session #270). Three prevention options: (1) Append-only writes for new entries (edits still need full-file write but adds are safe), (2) Line-count guard before any Write — if new content has fewer lines, warn before proceeding, (3) Git-based guard — compare against committed version, fewer lines = regression warning. Also consider: should the /todo skill use Edit tool for single-entry mutations instead of full-file Write? JSONL is line-based so targeted edits are feasible.
progress: Fixed across 8 commits (9aba28c7..c747a00b). Added scripts/lib/todos-mutations.js (pure helpers) + scripts/planning/todos-cli.js (locked CLI wrapper) with regression guard against silent drops. 56 tests pass. Skill wired via 93dc64af. Audit remediation (TOCTOU + scope comment + website-analysis snippet) in c747a00b. 3 follow-up todos filed: T34 (other JSONL writers), T35 (history diagnostic), T36 (schema strip).

title: Redesign hook state file tracking — split telemetry vs learning
description: Cross-locale sync destroys learning signal. Every day arriving at work forces discarding 12 dirty state files that are a mix of: Category A (telemetry / machine-local — .claude/hook-warnings.json, .claude/override-log.jsonl, .claude/state/consolidation.json, .claude/state/hook-warnings-log.jsonl + .archive, .claude/state/session-start-failures.json, .claude/state/warned-files.json, .claude/state/pending-refinements.jsonl) and Category B (cross-session learning — .claude/state/review-metrics.jsonl, .claude/state/learning-routes.jsonl, docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md, docs/LEARNING_METRICS.md). Both currently tracked in git; home/work locales produce divergent state that conflicts daily. 'Discard local' is the standard daily fix — which destroys Cat B signal with Cat A noise. Investigate: (1) classify all 12 files by reading writer code, (2) audit current .gitignore coverage, (3) evaluate options — gitignore Cat A entirely, per-locale subdirs (.claude/state/home/, /work/), merge-friendly JSONL append-only formats, or fix session-end reliability so nothing is ever dirty on arrival. Leading candidate: Option 1+3 hybrid — gitignore telemetry, keep learning tracked, investigate why learning files ever show dirty on arrival (if session-end commits correctly, they shouldn't). Related: T15 (gitignore audit, completed Session #270), T14 (learning system overhaul), feedback_commit_hook_state_files.md memory. Surfaced 2026-04-10 during cross-locale sync (work arrival).

title: Todo skill — invocation schema drift
description: scripts/reviews/dist/write-invocation.js schema now requires schema_version (number), completeness (full|partial|stub), and origin (object) fields not documented in .claude/skills/todo/SKILL.md 'Invocation Tracking' section. Current skill doc shows: node dist/write-invocation.js --data '{"skill":"todo","type":"skill","success":true,"context":{"action":"MENU_ACTION","todosActive":N}}' — that invocation now errors with 3 validation failures. Either (a) the skill doc is stale and needs updating to include the new required fields, or (b) the writer should fall back to defaults for legacy skill invocations. Likely affects other skills documenting invocation tracking with the same schema. Discovered 2026-04-10 during /todo add for T31. Non-blocking — only the invocation log write fails, the todo itself was added successfully. Check: grep for other skills with the same 'Invocation Tracking' snippet to find scope of drift.
progress: Fixed via dbec54db (writer-side auto-fill defaults for schema_version/completeness/origin) + db0cb2c4 (14-skill caller-side modernization sweep, zero legacy patterns remaining). All 18 skills now work with both legacy and modernized snippets. Canonical doc added to SKILL_STANDARDS.md in 5ef08109.

title: Fix PreToolUse hook: node command not found on Write/Edit
description: PreToolUse hook fires on Write/Edit and runs a node script, but the hook's /usr/bin/bash subshell doesn't inherit the user profile PATH, so fnm/nvm node is not found. Non-blocking — Writes still succeed — but every tool call emits 'node: command not found' stderr. Same class of issue as prior cross-locale session-start node failures. Fix options: prefix hook command with explicit node path, use 'bash -lc' to source profile, or call an ensure-fnm/ensure-node shim (check scripts/lib/). Investigate .claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.local.json for the hooks.PreToolUse entry that references node. Observed 2026-04-10 in Session #273 during memory writes and MEMORY.md edits after the skills-in-plans-are-tool-calls correction. Related: T31 (cross-locale hook state tracking).

title: Audit JSONL writers for full-rewrite data-loss bugs
description: T30 fixed /todo specifically. Other JSONL writers may have the same read-mutate-Write pattern: scan .planning/, .research/, .claude/state/ for files mutated via full-file Write tool overwrites (vs append-only via appendRecord). Candidates to check: .research/extraction-journal.jsonl, .claude/state/learning-routes.jsonl, .claude/state/pending-refinements.jsonl, .claude/state/hook-warnings.json, .planning/synthesis-consolidation/PLAN.md history. For each susceptible writer, route through a locked CLI helper or appendRecord. Surfaced 2026-04-10 during T30 fix (deferred per Q2 decision in todo-skill-fixes deep-plan).

title: Build audit-todos-history.js — diagnostic for historically-lost todo entries
description: T30 fixed forward protection but did not look backward. T26/T27/T28 were lost twice and recovered from git history. Build scripts/planning/audit-todos-history.js that walks git log of .planning/todos.jsonl, tracks max ID over time, and flags any commits where the max ID went backwards (indicating a silent drop). Output a report of any historical losses with the offending commit SHA and missing IDs. Diagnostic only — no auto-recovery. Surfaced 2026-04-10 during T30 fix (deferred per Q6 decision in todo-skill-fixes deep-plan).

title: InvocationRecord schema — strict context fields silently strip /todo's action+todosActive
description: Discovered 2026-04-10 during T32 writer-defaults testing. The /todo skill documents context fields {action, todosActive}, but neither is in scripts/reviews/lib/schemas/invocation.ts InvocationRecord context schema. Zod default behavior strips unknown keys, so writes succeed but those context fields are silently dropped — every /todo invocation record has context:{}. Either: (a) add action and todosActive to InvocationRecord context schema, (b) remove the field references from /todo SKILL.md, or (c) make context.passthrough() / .catchall() so unknown fields are preserved. The /todo invocation tracking is essentially useless until this is fixed. Same class of bug may affect other skills with skill-specific context fields.

description: Run /analyze on the GitNexus repo (https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus). Goal is to add it to the total repo reference map — the aggregate of all analyzed repos used as external-pattern prior art for the project. Integrate into .research/analysis/ and the extraction journal.

title: Hook ecosystem deep-dive — audit + pre-push failures + file-write pattern (cross-locale)
description: Multi-part hook investigation merging three concerns: (1) /hook-ecosystem-audit for the structured 20-category diagnostic paired with /brainstorm for improvement ideas; (2) Research pre-push hook failure patterns — categorize scoring quirks vs real failures vs timing races (e.g., persistent propagation-staged FAIL false positive seen across every commit in Session #274); (3) Better solution for hooks writing files during commits and pushes — hooks currently touch .claude/state/*, hook-warnings.json, LEARNING_METRICS.md etc., causing 5+ dirty state files per commit cycle. Options: queue updates, untracked location, auto-stage into same commit, session-end flush, or move to gitignored paths. CRITICAL: solution MUST work across work AND home locales (both machines have different fnm/nvm setups and different state). Related but distinct from T31 (telemetry vs learning classification).

title: Research data protection patterns — guard against rewrite/overwrite bugs
description: Broader than T30 (/todo specific) and T34 (JSONL writer sweep). title: Backfill Use-As-Is Verdict on prior product-repo analyses
description: /repo-analysis SKILL.md bumped to v4.6 (Session #278) — Creator View now requires Section 2b: Use-As-Is Verdict for product repos (taxonomic tag in {application, framework, tool-demo}). Retroactively apply to previously-analyzed product repos in .research/analysis/ so the corpus is consistent and /recall surfaces adoption intent. Scope: identify all existing analysis.json files where taxonomic tags include application/framework/tool-demo AND adoption_verdict field is missing. Likely candidates (tentative, verify via taxonomic tags): outline, docling, crawl4ai, jina-ai-reader, marker, vikparuchuri-marker, firecrawl, archivebox-archivebox, zedeus-nitter, MinerU, teng-lin-notebooklm-py, safishamsi-graphify, aws-media-extraction, bedrock-summarize-audio-video-text, bulk-transcribe-youtube-playlist, karpathy-autoresearch, youtube-transcript-api, lux-video-downloader, hkuds-cli-anything, viktoraxelsen-memskill, tesseract-quick, surya-quick. For each: (1) quick license/security/lock-in check, (2) Adopt/Trial/Extract-only/Avoid classification, (3) add Section 2b to creator-view.md + adoption fields to analysis.json + use-as-is row to summary.md. Can be parallelized via agents (1 per repo). Trigger for new runs: also re-check that repo-analysis Phase 6c tag proposal flags the product taxonomic category early enough that Phase 4 Creator View can route to Section 2b.

title: Fix /deep-plan template — status banner + per-step ✅ markers + plans:hygiene checker
description: Discovered Session #279: /deep-plan REFERENCE.md PLAN.md template (lines 119-154) does not include a status banner, per-step ✅ markers, or any hygiene mechanism. Both T29 and T28 PLAN files needed manual backfilling of completion markers because of this. Three-part fix: (1) Update .claude/skills/deep-plan/REFERENCE.md PLAN.md template to include top status banner placeholder, per-step header pattern with status placeholder, and a Status Summary section. (2) Update .claude/skills/deep-plan/SKILL.md to add a Critical Rule requiring plans MUST be updated to reflect step completion as work happens (same hygiene contract as TODOS.md vs todos.jsonl). (3) Add npm run plans:hygiene checker (parallel to roadmap:hygiene) that scans .planning/*/PLAN.md files for missing status banner, step headers lacking ✅/⏳/❌ markers, and plans where recent commits reference the plan but no step status changed. Full rationale in .planning/content-analysis-system/REMAINING_CAS_TASKS.md.

Do NOT adopt @optave/codegraph until empirical .md/.yaml edge support is verified.
